2605.12366.title: Classifier Context Rot: Monitor Performance Degrades with Context Length
2605.12366.paper_summary: Monitoring coding agents for dangerous behavior using language models requires classifying transcripts that often exceed 500K tokens, but prior agent monitoring benchmarks rarely contain transcripts longer than 100K tokens. We show that when used as classifiers, current frontier models fail to notice dangerous actions more often in longer transcripts. In particular, on a dataset that requires identifying when a coding agent takes a subtly dangerous action, Opus 4.6, GPT 5.4, and Gemini 3.1 miss these actions $2\times$ to $30\times$ more often when they occur after 800K tokens of benign activity than when they occur on their own. We also show that these weaknesses can be partially mitigated with prompting techniques such as periodic reminders throughout the transcript and may be mitigated further with better post-training. Monitor evaluations that do not consider long-context degradation are likely overestimating monitor performance.
2605.12366.paper_summary_zh: 監控編碼代理的危險行為使用語言模型需要對超過 500K 令牌的轉錄進行分類，但先前的代理監控基準很少包含長於 100K 令牌的轉錄。我們顯示，當作為分類器使用時，當前的前沿模型在較長的轉錄中更常忽略危險行為。特別是在一個需要識別編碼代理何時採取微妙危險行為的數據集上，Opus 4.6、GPT 5.4 和 Gemini 3.1 在 800K 令牌的良性活動之後發生這些行為的頻率比單獨發生時多出 $2\times$ 到 $30\times$。我們還顯示，這些弱點可以通過提示技術部分緩解，例如在整個轉錄過程中定期提醒，並且可能通過更好的後訓練進一步緩解。不考慮長上下文退化的監控評估可能會高估監控性能。
2605.12366.author: Sam Martin et.al.
